msDesc.head: Peter Lombard, Magna Glossatura on the Psalms, N. France (Paris?) ca. 1200

supportDesc.extent:
ii (modern paper) + 294 + ii (modern paper) leaves
315 210
Uncropped, leaving pricking and marginal directions to the rubricator, initialler, still clearly visible (esp. fols. 2v-3r)
supportDesc.collation: I 10, II-VIII 8, IX 10[8-10 cancelled, though only two stubs visible], X 8, X I2, XII-XV 8, XV I2, XVII-XVIII 8, XIX 8 [8 cancelled, verso of 7 blank], XX-XXXVII 8, XXXVIII 4 [lacks 1 and 2, text missing]. Each quire is systematically marked either (1) with a symbol (such as a cross) and letters from ‘a’ to ‘d’ or ‘e’ in the bottom right hand corner of the rectos in the first half of the quire with the symbol alone on the recto of the central bifolium of each quire or (2) in the later quires, with a letter to which four of different sigla are added in succession. In addition, each quire is numbered with a roman numeral on the verso of the last folio and given a flourished catchword, both in the lower inner margin. Quire numbering starts again at I on fol. 148v (last folio of the first quire of part 2).

layoutDesc.layout: Written space: fols 1r-2v (Prologue), 210 x 140mm, for remainder of MS even where text format changes, 215 140 Ruled in lead in 2 columns of 40 lines for fols 1r-2v, 47-48 lines for the rest of the MS; written above the top line. Text of the Psalms written every other line, taking up full height of those 2 lines, commentary written every line. Running titles added in a variety of hands at different times.

handDesc.handNote: Fols. 2v-73v and 141r-294r, good early Gothic minuscule.

decoDesc.decoNote: Fine penwork initials in red and blue.

additions.p: Contemporary annotations noted fols. 74v, 105v, 109v, 140r; 74v perhaps in French.

binding.p: English binding of the 3rd quarter of the 18th century. There is a rust stain in the lower margin of fol. 1r, and another in centre of the upper margins of the last two folios, suggesting a chain staple on the back board.

history.provenance: Throckmorton family at Coughton Court, Warwickshire. Fol. 1r, upper margin. ‘Dni Robti Throck Baronetti’, third baronet; lower margin, ‘Biblioth. Weston’.

Inside front board: bookplate of Robert Throckmorton, 4th Baronet (1702-91): Jacobean armorial style, Throckmorton quartered with Yate, acanthus surround, helmet with eagle crest, motto on scroll beneath – ‘virtus sola nobilitas’ (Franks 29425; ‘Prob. engraved by S. Pomarede’.) Inscription ‘Sr. Robt. Throckmorton Bar.t’ beneath
Offered for sale, Christie's, 20 Dec. 1972, as part of lots 204, 206-211, but withdrawn before the sale.
Sold to the National Trust in 1991.
history.acquisition: Deposited by the National Trust in 2026.

recordHist.source: Abbreviated from a draft description by Suzanne Reynolds.
